pages.title: MPLP — Multi-Agent Lifecycle Protocol
pages.description: MPLP is a vendor-neutral lifecycle protocol for AI agent systems. This website provides discovery and positioning only. Repository and documentation provide the authoritative documentation chain. Validation Lab provides the adjudication surface.

pages.title: Architecture | MPLP — Multi-Agent Lifecycle Protocol
pages.description: High-level architecture overview for MPLP. Formal protocol requirements live in the documentation and repository.

pages.title: Blog
pages.description: MPLP Protocol news, updates, and technical articles.

pages.title: Conformance Model (Informative) | MPLP Specification
pages.description: Discovery page for MPLP conformance-related materials. Formal conformance references live in documentation and Validation Lab surfaces.

pages.title: Ecosystem | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: Published package surfaces, developer tools, and reference runtimes that support adoption of the MPLP protocol standard.

pages.title: FAQ | MPLP — Multi-Agent Lifecycle Protocol
pages.description: Frequently asked questions about MPLP, including its formal definition, boundaries, evaluation guidance, and AI citation references.

pages.title: Golden Flows | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: Discovery page for protocol-side Golden Flow scenarios. Formal definitions live in the documentation and repository tests.

pages.title: Single Agent – Happy Path | MPLP Golden Flows
pages.description: Website summary of MPLP core published-boundary scenario: The baseline single-agent workflow. Agent receives context, generates a minimal 2-step plan, executes, and produces trace.

pages.title: Single Agent – Large Plan | MPLP Golden Flows
pages.description: Website summary of MPLP core published-boundary scenario: Volumetric validation with 20+ steps. Tests protocol handling of large execution plans.

pages.title: Single Agent – With Tools | MPLP Golden Flows
pages.description: Website summary of MPLP core published-boundary scenario: Tool integration via agent_role field. Validates protocol semantics for tool-enabled workflows.

pages.title: Single Agent with LLM Enrichment | MPLP Golden Flows
pages.description: Website summary of MPLP core published-boundary scenario: AEL (Action Execution Loop) integration. Validates LLM-enriched plan generation.

pages.title: Single Agent with Confirm Required | MPLP Golden Flows
pages.description: Website summary of MPLP core published-boundary scenario: Multi-round approval workflow. Validates Confirm module integration.

pages.title: MAP Turn-Taking Session | MPLP Golden Flows
pages.description: Website summary of MPLP profile-level flow scenario: Multi-Agent Protocol with turn-taking. Two agents collaborate with role rotation.

pages.title: MAP Broadcast Fan-out | MPLP Golden Flows
pages.description: Website summary of MPLP profile-level flow scenario: Multi-Agent Protocol with broadcast. Parallel dispatch to multiple agents.

pages.title: SA Basic Execution | MPLP Golden Flows
pages.description: Website summary of MPLP profile-level flow scenario: Single-Agent (SA) profile baseline. Single-step execution with full lifecycle.

pages.title: SA Multi-Step Evaluation | MPLP Golden Flows
pages.description: Website summary of MPLP profile-level flow scenario: SA profile with multi-step plan. Validates step sequencing in SA mode.

pages.title: EU AI Act Alignment | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: Reference mapping between EU AI Act high-risk AI system requirements (Articles 9–15) and MPLP protocol mechanisms. MPLP is not an auditing authority and does not issue attestations.

pages.title: Evidence Chain | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: Plan, Confirm, and Trace form the MPLP Evidence Chain — the governance mechanism that supports auditability and governance assurance in agent systems.

pages.title: Governed Stack | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: How MPLP relates to MCP, agent frameworks, and runtimes. These are governed objects in a lifecycle, not competitors.

pages.title: ISO/IEC 42001 Alignment | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: Reference mapping between ISO/IEC 42001 AI management system objectives and MPLP protocol mechanisms. MPLP is not an auditing authority and does not issue attestations.

pages.title: NIST AI RMF Alignment | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: Reference mapping between NIST AI Risk Management Framework functions and MPLP protocol modules. MPLP is not reviewed or validated by NIST.

pages.title: Governance Overview | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: Discovery overview of MPLP governance. Repository governance records and documentation references provide current authoritative detail.

pages.title: Agentic State Sovereignty | MPLP Positioning
pages.description: MPLP positioning on agent state ownership: State belongs to the Protocol, execution belongs to the Runtime.

pages.title: Semantic Drift Control | MPLP Positioning
pages.description: MPLP positioning on semantic drift: Drift is a Protocol Violation, not a Model Behavior Issue.

pages.title: Kernel Duties
pages.description: The 11 kernel duties that describe lifecycle governance semantics in the MPLP protocol. Defines cross-cutting responsibilities across the protocol specification.

pages.title: Protocol Modules | MPLP Standard
pages.description: The ten normative modules that define the MPLP protocol standard, covering execution semantics, governance, extensibility, and protocol boundaries.

pages.title: Collab Module | MPLP Protocol Specification
pages.description: Defines the normative coordination contract and multi-agent execution semantics of the Collab module in the MPLP protocol.

pages.title: Confirm Module | MPLP Protocol Specification
pages.description: Defines supporting protocol semantics for confirmation and governance triggers within the MPLP standard.

pages.title: Context Module | MPLP Protocol Specification
pages.description: Defines the normative semantics, constraints, and interaction model of the Context module in the MPLP protocol.

pages.title: Core Module | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: Defines protocol metadata, invariants, and structural boundaries within the MPLP standard.

pages.title: Dialog Module | MPLP Protocol Specification
pages.description: Defines supporting protocol semantics for structured interaction within the MPLP standard.

pages.title: Extension Module | MPLP Protocol Specification
pages.description: Defines supporting protocol semantics for safe extensibility boundaries within the MPLP standard.

pages.title: Network Module | MPLP Protocol
pages.description: Defines the semantic boundary between MPLP protocol semantics and external communication layers.

pages.title: Plan Module | MPLP Protocol Specification
pages.description: Defines the normative execution structure, reasoning semantics, and constraints of the Plan module in the MPLP protocol.

pages.title: Role Module | MPLP Protocol Specification
pages.description: Defines the normative identity contract, authorization boundaries, and capability semantics of the Role module in the MPLP protocol.

pages.title: Trace Module | MPLP Protocol Specification
pages.description: Defines the evidence-grade audit layer, execution history, and observability semantics of the Trace module in the MPLP protocol.

pages.title: POSIX Analogy
pages.description: Understanding the Multi-Agent Lifecycle Protocol through the POSIX analogy. A non-normative conceptual explainer.

pages.title: References | MPLP — Multi-Agent Lifecycle Protocol
pages.description: Citation contexts and external references for MPLP. Repository and documentation remain the authoritative documentation chain.

pages.title: Specification Entry
pages.description: Official entry point for the Multi-Agent Lifecycle Protocol (MPLP) specification. Access normative documentation and source schemas.

pages.title: Validation Lab — MPLP
pages.description: Discovery guide to MPLP Validation Lab. This website page is pointer-oriented; current Lab references and adjudication outputs live in the documentation and Lab surfaces.

pages.title: What is MPLP? | Multi-Agent Lifecycle Protocol
pages.description: MPLP is a vendor-neutral lifecycle protocol for AI agent systems. This is the canonical website definition anchor.

pages.title: Why MPLP? | The Multi-Agent Lifecycle Protocol
pages.description: The MPLP specification describes a lifecycle model for multi-agent systems. Learn about the coordination challenges MPLP addresses.
